originalTitle: Desperate for food drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: Amazing Tree Helps Kids in Africa!
content:
Hello, Super Explorers! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share a very important story from our big continent, Africa. Get ready to learn about an amazing tree that is helping lots of people, especially kids, when food is hard to find.

No Rain, No Food

Imagine your garden at home, but for a very, very long time, no rain comes down. What would happen to the plants? They would get very dry and sad, right? Well, that's what has been happening in some parts of Africa, in countries like Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. When there is no rain for a long time, we call it a 'drought'. A drought makes it very hard for farmers to grow yummy food like maize, beans, or vegetables. Animals also struggle because there's no fresh grass or water to drink. This means that many families, and especially children, don't have enough to eat. It makes their tummies rumble, and they can feel very weak and sad.

The Wonderful Gingerbread Tree!

But guess what? Nature has given us a special helper! In Kenya, when other foods are hard to find, people are turning to a wonderful tree called the 'gingerbread tree'. Isn't that a fun name? It's also known as the 'Doum Palm'. This tree is like a superhero! It can grow even when there isn't much rain. Its fruit looks a bit like a small, round mango, but when you break it open, it has a yummy, sweet taste that reminds some people of gingerbread! People eat the fruit, and it helps them feel fuller and gives them energy when there isn't much else around. It's like finding a secret snack in a very dry place. The gingerbread tree has been a friend to people for many, many years, especially when times are tough. Did you know? The gingerbread tree is super useful! Not only do people eat its fruit, but they also use its leaves to make baskets, mats, and even roofs for their homes! It's truly a gift from nature.

Why is there no food?

Let's talk a bit more about why there's not enough food. It's all because of the drought we mentioned. When the sun shines too hot and there are no clouds to bring rain for many months, the ground becomes super dry and hard, like baked clay. Plants need water to grow big and strong, just like you need water to drink every day. Without water, crops like corn and wheat cannot grow. And if crops don't grow, farmers can't harvest food to feed their families or to sell. This makes it very, very difficult for everyone. Imagine not having water to drink or to grow food for a whole year! That's how serious a drought can be. It makes people feel hungry and sick, and they don't have the energy to play or go to school. Did you know? A single raindrop is made of millions of tiny water molecules! We need lots and lots of them to make enough rain for our plants and rivers.

People Helping People

Sometimes, when big problems happen, kind people and groups come together to help. There's a special group called Oxfam. They are like a team of helpful heroes who work to make sure people have enough food, clean water, and medicine when they face big problems like droughts. Oxfam says that a *huge* number of people are facing extreme hunger in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. Can you guess how many? It's about 26 million people! That's like all the kids in hundreds and hundreds of schools put together! It's a very big number of friends, neighbours, and families who need help. These helpful heroes, like Oxfam, bring special food that gives energy, clean water to drink, and sometimes even seeds so farmers can try to plant again when the rain finally comes. They work very hard to make sure people don't go hungry. Did you know? Many children your age around the world help others by collecting old toys or clothes for charity, or by raising money to buy food for those in need! Every little bit of help counts.

Our African Neighbours

Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia are all beautiful countries in East Africa. They have amazing animals like giraffes and elephants, big, open spaces, and warm, friendly people. But sometimes, nature can be a bit tricky, and they face challenges like these droughts. It's important for us to know about what's happening to our neighbours in Africa. Even though they are far away, we are all part of the big human family. We hope that soon, the rains will come, the crops will grow, and everyone will have plenty of delicious food to eat, thanks to kind helpers and amazing trees like the gingerbread tree! Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world and has some incredible ancient churches carved right out of rock! It's a place full of history and wonder.

What Can We Do?

Even as kids, we can help! We can learn about what's happening, appreciate the food we have, and remember to never waste it. We can also be kind and caring to everyone around us. Knowing about these things helps us understand the world better. Let's hope for lots of rain and happy, full tummies for everyone in Africa soon!
summary: Many people, especially children, in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia are very hungry because there has been no rain for a long time, but a special 'gingerbread tree' is helping them find food.
whyMatters: This is important because many kids and families don't have enough food to eat, and it shows how a special tree and kind people are working together to help them stay strong and healthy.

originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: A Story About Neighbors: Chad and Sudan Want Peace
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, wonderful friends! Your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa is here to share an important story with you. We're going to talk about two countries that are neighbors, just like you might have a friend living next door. These countries are called Chad and Sudan. Sometimes, even good neighbors can have a tricky time, and it's our job to understand what's happening and why everyone is working hard to keep things safe and peaceful. Africa is a giant, beautiful continent with so many amazing countries and people. Today, we're zooming in on two special countries right next to each other. They share a very long border, which is like an invisible line on the ground that tells us where one country ends and another begins. Imagine drawing a line in the sand, but this line is so big it goes for hundreds of kilometers!

Two Neighbor Countries: Chad and Sudan

First, let's meet Chad! Chad is a lovely country in the middle of Africa. It has a big lake called Lake Chad, and many amazing animals live there. Its capital city is N'Djamena. Right next to Chad is Sudan, another big country with a famous river called the Nile. Khartoum is the capital of Sudan. People in both countries are often friends, sharing stories, trading things, and visiting each other. Did you know? Chad is sometimes called 'The Dead Heart of Africa' because it's so far from the ocean, but it's full of life and beautiful landscapes!

Something Sad Happened

Now, for the serious part of our story. Something very sad happened recently in Chad. Some people were together, feeling sad because someone they knew had passed away. When people gather to remember someone who has died, we call them 'mourners'. It's a time for quietness and remembering. But while these mourners were together, something called a 'drone' flew over and caused a problem. It was very scary, and sadly, 17 people lost their lives. This made many people in Chad very, very sad and worried.

What is a Drone?

You might be wondering, what is a drone? A drone is like a small flying robot! Some drones are used for fun, like taking cool pictures from up high, or delivering packages. But some drones are used by armies, and they can sometimes carry things that can cause harm. In this sad story, it seems a drone from Sudan caused the problem in Chad. It was a mistake, or something went wrong, but it caused great sadness. Did you know? The very first drones were invented over 100 years ago! They've changed a lot since then, and now many people have small drones to fly for hobbies!

Chad's President Steps In

When something sad like this happens, the leaders of a country need to act quickly to keep everyone safe. In Chad, the President is Mr. Idris Mahamat Déby. He is like the main leader, making big decisions for the whole country. President Déby heard about what happened and he knew he had to do something important. He wants to make sure nothing like this happens again, and that the people of Chad feel safe.

Making Borders Safe

One of the first things President Déby did was to make sure the 'border' between Chad and Sudan was closed. Remember that invisible line? Well, closing the border means that for a little while, people cannot easily go from Chad into Sudan, or from Sudan into Chad. It's like closing the front gate to your garden if there's something you need to fix inside, just to keep everyone safe until things are better. This helps keep things calm and helps the country's protectors (the army) keep an eye on things. Did you know? Some borders have special flags or signs, but many are just lines on a map that countries agree on!

The Army is Ready!

President Déby also told Chad's army to be on 'high alert'. What does 'high alert' mean? It means the army needs to be extra, extra ready. Like superheroes waiting for a signal, they are prepared to protect their country and its people if anything bad tries to happen again. Being on high alert shows that Chad is serious about keeping its citizens safe. He also warned Sudan that Chad would 'retaliate' if something bad happened again. 'Retaliate' means to do something back. It's like if someone keeps accidentally bumping into you, you might say, 'Please stop, or I will have to move away!' It's a way of saying, 'We are strong, and we will protect ourselves if we are harmed'.

Why is This Important?

This story shows us how important it is for countries to talk to each other and live in peace. When things get difficult between neighbors, it can make people worried. Leaders like President Déby work very hard to find solutions and make sure that everyone can live safely and happily in their homes and countries. Safety is a big job for leaders, and they take it very seriously. Did you know? Many countries in Africa work together in groups to help each other and keep the peace!

Hoping for Peace and Smiles

Even though sad things can happen, the goal is always to have peace. Peace means everyone can live without fear, play, learn, and grow up strong. Chad and Sudan are neighbors, and like all neighbors, they hope to live together peacefully for a long, long time. We hope that soon, everyone in Chad and Sudan can feel safe and happy again, and that smiles will return to every face.
summary: Chad is making sure its people are safe after a sad drone incident from Sudan, closing its border and getting its army ready, hoping for peace between the two neighbor countries.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how important it is for countries that are neighbors to be careful and talk to each other, so everyone can stay safe and live in peace.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
content: Caf executive committee member Augustin Senghor says the decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Afcon title is "unacceptable, abject and we have to denounce it".
